content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
January 9, 1990


Rita Walters
Board of Education
City of Los Angeles
450 North Grand Avenue
Los Angeles, CA  90051

RE:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-666

Dear Ms. Walters:

     You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

FACTS

     You have been advised by counsel that recent court actions
have affected FPPC regulations relating to the utilization of
campaign funds possessed prior to the effective date of
Proposition 73.  You have established two separate bank accounts
so that contributions received prior to January 1, 1989 may be
distinguished from contributions received after this date.

QUESTION

     What are the permissible uses of campaign funds received
prior to January 1, 1989?

CONCLUSION

     Candidates and officeholders may use all funds received prior
to January 1, 1989 to support their own candidacy.  Therefore, the
funds you raised prior to January 1, 1989, may be used to support
your candidacy for any elective office.  Enclosed is FPPC advice
letter I-89-378 which provides the basis for this conclusion and
an analysis.

     Effective January 1, 1990, certain restrictions on the use of
campaign funds were added to the Act.  (Chapter 1452, Stats.
1989.)  Previously, these provisions were contained in the

_________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Rita Walters
Page 2


California Elections Code. In general, the new provisions
(Sections 85800-85807) require that expenditures of campaign funds
be reasonably related to a political, legislative or governmental
purpose. Enclosed is a fact sheet which explains the provisions.

Finally, please note that, pursuant to Section 85304, a
candidate may not transfer his or her campaign funds to another
candidate or to a committee which will use the funds to support or
oppose candidates for office.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662 if you
need additional assistance on this question.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

EFDC

Nov 20 8 05 AM '09

BOARD OF EDUCATION CITY OF LOS ANGELES
450 NORTH GRAND AVENUE / PO. BOX 3307, LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90051
TEL. NO. (213) 625-6382

RITA WAITERS
MEMBER

MEMBERS OF THE BOARD
WARREN FURUTANI
JACKIE GOLDBERG
JULIE KORENSTEIN
LETICIA QUEZADA
MARK SLAUKIN
ROBERTA WEINTRAUB

November 14, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Utilization Of Campaign Funds Received Prior
To The Effective Date Of Proposition 73

To The Commission:

I have been advised by District counsel that recent developments
in court have affected the FPPC's regulation related to the
utilization of campaign funds possessed prior to the effective
date of Proposition 73.

As a result of your regulation, I have established two separate
bank accounts, so that contributions possessed as of the effective
date of Proposition 73 may be distinguished from contributions
received after. (I have filed a notice of candidacy to enable the
receipt of campaign funds.)

As I understand it, a summary judgment was granted in the United
States District Court for the Eastern District of California.
Your written advice is requested as to the continuing validity of
your regulation on the subject and any other advice that you may
be able to give me with respect to the utilization of these funds.

Should you have any questions related to this request, please
feel free to contact me.

Very truly yours,

Rita Waiters

RW:ew

cc: Richard K. Mason, Esq.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 17, 1989


Rita Walters
Board of Education City of
Los angeles
450 North Grand Avenue
Los Angeles, CA  90051

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-666

Dear Ms. Walters:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on November 20, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible] Pritchard
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Chief Technical Assistance
                                        and Analysis Division
